2604.26136.title: One Voice, Many Tongues: Cross-Lingual Voice Cloning for Scientific Speech
2604.26136.paper_summary: Preserving a speaker's voice identity while generating speech in a different language remains a fundamental challenge in spoken language technology, particularly in specialized domains such as scientific communication. In this paper, we address this challenge through our system submission to the International Conference on Spoken Language Translation (IWSLT 2026), the Cross-Lingual Voice Cloning shared task. First, we evaluate several state-of-the-art voice cloning models for cross-lingual speech generation of scientific texts in Arabic, Chinese, and French. Then, we build voice cloning systems based on the OmniVoice foundation model. We employ data augmentation via multi-model ensemble distillation from the ACL 60/60 corpus. We investigate the effect of using this synthetic data for fine-tuning, demonstrating consistent improvements in intelligibility (WER and CER) across languages while preserving speaker similarity.
2604.26136.paper_summary_zh: 保留講者的聲音身份，同時在不同語言中生成語音仍然是口語語言技術中的一個基本挑戰，特別是在科學交流等專業領域中。<lb/>在本文中，我們通過向國際口語語言翻譯會議（IWSLT 2026）的系統提交，針對這一挑戰進行探討，這是跨語言聲音克隆的共享任務。<lb/>首先，我們評估了幾個最先進的聲音克隆模型，用於阿拉伯語、中文和法語的科學文本的跨語言語音生成。<lb/>然後，我們基於OmniVoice基礎模型構建聲音克隆系統。<lb/>我們通過從ACL 60/60語料庫進行多模型集成蒸餾來進行數據增強。<lb/>我們調查了使用這些合成數據進行微調的效果，顯示在保持講者相似性的同時，各語言的可懂度（WER和CER）均有穩定的改善。
2604.26136.author: Amanuel Gizachew Abebe et.al.
